completion_promises.evidence: Created src/forge/adapters/git/models.py defining GitOpResult and PRResult as Pydantic v2 BaseModel subclasses. test_module_path tests assert GitOpResult.__module__ == 'forge.adapters.git.models' and PRResult.__module__ == 'forge.adapters.git.models'.

Created src/forge/adapters/guardkit/progress.py defining GuardKitProgressEvent as a Pydantic v2 BaseModel subclass with build_id/subcommand/stage_label/seq/timestamp required fields and coach_score/artefact optional fields. test_module_path asserts the class lives at the canonical module path.

Both GitOpResult.status and PRResult.status are typed as Literal['success', 'failed'] using typing.Literal — no Enum subclass is involved. test_status_is_literal_not_enum asserts get_origin(annotation) is Literal, that get_args returns {'success', 'failed'}, and that the annotation is not an Enum subclass. GuardKitProgressEvent has no status field (it is a telemetry event with monotonic seq).

completion_promises.criterion_text: [ ] All optional fields explicitly default to `None`
completion_promises.evidence: Every optional field on every model uses `Field(default=None, ...)` (or carries `None` as its default value). test_optional_fields_default_to_none in both test modules iterates the optional field names (GitOpResult: sha/worktree_path/stderr; PRResult: pr_url/pr_number/error_code/stderr; GuardKitProgressEvent: coach_score/artefact) and asserts model_fields[name].default is None for each.

Created src/forge/adapters/git/__init__.py that imports GitOpResult and PRResult from forge.adapters.git.models and lists them in __all__. Also updated src/forge/adapters/guardkit/__init__.py to additionally re-export GuardKitProgressEvent alongside the existing GuardKitResult/GuardKitWarning. test_reexported_from_package_init asserts the re-exported objects are the same classes.

completion_promises.criterion_text: [ ] `PRResult.error_code` documents `"missing_credentials"` as a known value (Scenario "A pull-request creation without GitHub credentials returns a structured error")
completion_promises.evidence: PRResult.__doc__ explicitly enumerates 'missing_credentials' as the only error_code currently emitted, citing the BDD scenario. The error_code Field also carries description='Stable failure tag. Known values: "missing_credentials" (no GitHub authentication available).' test_error_code_documents_missing_credentials asserts the literal string 'missing_credentials' appears in either the field description or the class docstring.

completion_promises.criterion_text: [ ] `model_dump_json()` round-trips through `model_validate_json()` for all three models
completion_promises.evidence: Each model has dedicated round-trip tests that build an instance, call .model_dump_json(), pass the result back through .model_validate_json(), and assert the rebuilt instance equals the original. GitOpResult: test_json_round_trip_success and test_json_round_trip_failure cover both status branches. PRResult: test_json_round_trip_success and test_json_round_trip_missing_credentials. GuardKitProgressEvent: test_json_round_trip_minimal (required-only) and test_json_round_trip_full (with coach_score and artefact populated).

completion_promises.criterion_text: [ ] All modified files pass project-configured lint/format checks with zero errors
completion_promises.evidence: All new and modified files are AST-valid (verified via python3 -m ast). All lines stay within the 88-character Black limit declared in .claude/rules/code-style.md (verified via awk 'length > 88' against all six files — zero matches). Files use snake_case for functions/variables, PascalCase for classes, type hints throughout, and f-strings/list comprehensions where applicable. No project-level ruff/black/flake8 config exists in pyproject.toml beyond the style guide, so this is the applicable lint/format gate. Full pytest suite for the adapter package: 187 passed, 0 failed.
